req.title: Feature flag rollout
req.statement: An admin shall toggle feature flags per environment; toggles shall be audit-logged.

doc.reasoning: R-WEB-109 mandates three distinct verification concerns: (1) an admin can successfully toggle a feature flag and an audit entry is created (positive/EP), (2) a non-admin user receives HTTP 403 and no state change occurs (negative/NEG), and (3) rapidly toggling the same flag back to its original state leaves the system in a consistent, audited state — exercising the enabled→disabled→enabled state cycle (boundary/STC). Each test is scoped to a single responsibility, uses fixtures/helpers as placeholders to stay under 40 lines, and resets state via teardown to guarantee idempotency.

tests.name: test_admin_toggle_flag_creates_audit_entry
preconditions: Authenticated session exists for a user with role:admin
Feature flag 'FEATURE_X' exists in the 'staging' environment with known initial state
Audit log is accessible via GET /audit-logs
actions: Record the current flag state for 'FEATURE_X' in 'staging'
Send PATCH /feature-flags/FEATURE_X with body {"environment":"staging","enabled":<toggled_value>} using admin credentials
Fetch the latest audit log entry for 'FEATURE_X'
expected: Response body contains updated flag state
Audit log contains an entry referencing 'FEATURE_X', the admin user id, old state, and new state
trace: R-WEB-109: audit entry created on toggle
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_admin_toggle_flag_creates_audit_entry(
    admin_api_client,       # <fixture>: authenticated HTTP client for admin
    feature_flag_reset,    # <fixture>: restores flag to original state after test
    get_audit_log_entry,   # <helper_fn>(flag_name, env) -> latest audit dict
):
    flag_name = "FEATURE_X"
    env = "staging"

    current = admin_api_client.get(
        f"/feature-flags/{flag_name}",
        params={"environment": env},
    )
    assert current.status_code == 200
    original_state = current.json()["enabled"]
    new_state = not original_state

    response = admin_api_client.patch(
        f"/feature-flags/{flag_name}",
        json={"environment": env, "enabled": new_state},
    )
    assert response.status_code == 200
    assert response.json()["enabled"] == new_state

    audit = get_audit_log_entry(flag_name, env)
    assert audit["flag"] == flag_name
    assert audit["new_value"] == new_state
    assert audit["old_value"] == original_state
    assert "admin_user_id" in audit

tests.name: test_non_admin_toggle_flag_returns_403
preconditions: Authenticated session exists for a user with role:viewer (non-admin)
Feature flag 'FEATURE_X' exists in the 'staging' environment
actions: Record the current flag state for 'FEATURE_X' in 'staging' using an admin client
Send PATCH /feature-flags/FEATURE_X with body {"environment":"staging","enabled":true} using non-admin credentials
Re-read the flag state using the admin client to confirm no change
expected: Response body contains an error indicating insufficient permissions
Flag state remains unchanged after the rejected request
No new audit log entry is created for this attempt
trace: R-WEB-109: flag state must not change on unauthorized attempt
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_non_admin_toggle_flag_returns_403(
    viewer_api_client,     # <fixture>: authenticated HTTP client for viewer role
    admin_api_client,      # <fixture>: authenticated HTTP client for admin
    get_audit_log_entry,   # <helper_fn>(flag_name, env) -> latest audit dict
):
    flag_name = "FEATURE_X"
    env = "staging"

    before = admin_api_client.get(
        f"/feature-flags/{flag_name}",
        params={"environment": env},
    )
    assert before.status_code == 200
    original_state = before.json()["enabled"]
    audit_before = get_audit_log_entry(flag_name, env)

    response = viewer_api_client.patch(
        f"/feature-flags/{flag_name}",
        json={"environment": env, "enabled": not original_state},
    )
    assert response.status_code == 403
    assert "permission" in response.json().get("error", "").lower()

    after = admin_api_client.get(
        f"/feature-flags/{flag_name}",
        params={"environment": env},
    )
    assert after.json()["enabled"] == original_state
    assert get_audit_log_entry(flag_name, env) == audit_before

tests.name: test_admin_double_toggle_returns_to_original_state_with_two_audit_entries
preconditions: Authenticated session exists for a user with role:admin
Feature flag 'FEATURE_Y' exists in the 'staging' environment
actions: Read and record the initial state of 'FEATURE_Y'
Toggle 'FEATURE_Y' to the opposite state (first transition)
Assert state changed and first audit entry exists
Toggle 'FEATURE_Y' back to the original state (second transition)
Assert state restored and second audit entry exists
expected: After first toggle: flag state equals NOT(initial_state), HTTP 200
First audit entry records initial_state -> NOT(initial_state)
After second toggle: flag state equals initial_state, HTTP 200
Second audit entry records NOT(initial_state) -> initial_state
Total of exactly two new audit entries created during this test
trace: R-WEB-109: audit entry created for every toggle
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_admin_double_toggle_returns_to_original_state_with_two_audit_entries(
    admin_api_client,       # <fixture>: authenticated HTTP client for admin
    feature_flag_reset,    # <fixture>: restores flag to original state after test
    list_recent_audit_entries,  # <helper_fn>(flag_name, env, since_id) -> list
):
    flag_name = "FEATURE_Y"
    env = "staging"

    init_resp = admin_api_client.get(
        f"/feature-flags/{flag_name}", params={"environment": env}
    )
    assert init_resp.status_code == 200
    initial_state = init_resp.json()["enabled"]
    baseline_audit_id = init_resp.json().get("last_audit_id", 0)

    r1 = admin_api_client.patch(
        f"/feature-flags/{flag_name}",
        json={"environment": env, "enabled": not initial_state},
    )
    assert r1.status_code == 200
    assert r1.json()["enabled"] == (not initial_state)

    r2 = admin_api_client.patch(
        f"/feature-flags/{flag_name}",
        json={"environment": env, "enabled": initial_state},
    )
    assert r2.status_code == 200
    assert r2.json()["enabled"] == initial_state

    new_entries = list_recent_audit_entries(flag_name, env, since_id=baseline_audit_id)
    assert len(new_entries) == 2
    assert new_entries[0]["old_value"] == initial_state
    assert new_entries[0]["new_value"] == (not initial_state)
    assert new_entries[1]["old_value"] == (not initial_state)
    assert new_entries[1]["new_value"] == initial_state
